summary: End-to-end testing of the new admin-only Magazijn Checkin feature. Backend: 15/15 pytest tests passed in /app/backend/tests/test_checkin.py (auth: 401 anon / 403 user / 403 donateur; happy paths with single and multi-item incl. optional description; validation: 404 invalid org, 404 non-validated org, 422 empty items, 422 zero/negative weight, 422 description >200 chars; stats: checkins_count + totals.checkin_kg + by_org_checkin sorted-desc aggregation verified before/after POST; available-periods includes checkin year; year filter excludes other years). Frontend: 27/27 Playwright assertions passed (anonymous → /login, non-admin lotte → /, admin-link-checkout + admin-link-checkin in QR block, /checkin reached via QR link, Step1 Volgende disabled→enabled flow, suggestions after 2+ chars, Step2 add-item clears weight+description, description shown italic only when entered, Step3 summary org+per-material grouping (Hout 5.5+2.0=7.5)+notes section+total 10.75kg, Step4 success with org name + 10.75kg + 'Incheckin geregistreerd!' title, Restart returns to Step 1 with cleared inputs, AdminPanel Statistieken tab shows admin-stats-checkin section with count/kg widgets and top-orgs table). Donateur fixture used password 'test1234' from test_credentials.md (review_request stated 'User123!' which is incorrect).

minor.issue: Spec says totalWeightKg should be rounded to 2 decimals, but backend uses round(total, 3) (routes/checkin.py line 20). For sums with more than 2 decimals (e.g. 1.234 + 2.5 + 0.5 = 4.234), the response returns 4.234 instead of 4.23. Frontend displays via .toFixed(2) so users don't see the discrepancy, but the API contract diverges from spec.

action_items: (Minor) Decide whether totalWeightKg should be rounded to 2 decimals (spec) or 3 decimals (current). If 2, change round(..., 3) → round(..., 2) in routes/checkin.py and round per-item weightKg too.
(Doc) Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md or the review_request: donateur donna@inlimbo.be password is 'test1234' (per credentials file) — review_request listed 'User123!' which fails.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/checkin.py: org status check 'validated'/'active' is correctly aligned with the validated_only filter used elsewhere; non-existent and non-validated orgs both return 404 with the same message — fine for security but could be split into 404/400 for clarity.
Pydantic CheckinItem.description Field(None, max_length=200) is correctly enforced (422 verified). CheckinCreate.items min_length=1 enforced (422 verified).

admin.py /admin/stats correctly aggregates checkins separately from checkouts — by_org_checkin uses organisationName stored on the doc (no extra org lookup needed). Year filter date_filter uses createdAt prefix string compare which works because createdAt is ISO 8601.
Frontend Checkin.jsx: organisationId required → only enabled after suggestion click; description trimmed and sent as null when empty (matches backend Optional[str]). Clean reset implementation.
test_credentials: Admin admin@inlimbo.be / Admin123! · User lotte@atelier-brussel.example / User123! · Donateur donna@inlimbo.be / test1234 (NOT 'User123!' as stated in review_request)
seed_data_creation: Tests created ~5 checkin documents in db.checkins (Atelier Brussel + first validated org). Items marked with 'TEST_' prefix in description. These accumulate in stats but are harmless and small.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The new checkin feature is fully working both at API and UI level. Reusable test file at /app/backend/tests/test_checkin.py covers auth, happy paths, validation, stats aggregation, and indirectly mongo persistence. The only spec deviation is totalWeightKg rounded to 3 decimals instead of 2 — not user-visible since frontend formats with .toFixed(2). admin@inlimbo.be navbar greets 'Samuel' (likely the seeded admin firstName) — minor and unrelated to this feature.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found that required RCA.
